title: Simera Sense to provide imagers for Canary Islands constellation
summary: Simera Sense, a Belgian company, secured a contract to supply multispectral imagers for a small constellation aimed at monitoring wildfires, volcanic activity, and other regional priorities in the Canary Islands. The constellation will focus on addressing critical environmental and safety needs for the Spanish archipelago.

title: SpaceX satellite wins test Pentagon’s commitment to competition
summary: SpaceX's satellite contract has drawn congressional attention as the Pentagon prioritizes competition in military satellite deployments. The move highlights scrutiny over SpaceX's growing dominance in the space industry.

title: Isar Aerospace signs agreement for Canadian launch site
summary: Isar Aerospace has signed an agreement to establish a Canadian launch site for its Spectrum rocket, which is tied to a submarine deal with the Canadian military.

title: Blue Origin continues work on lunar landers during recovery from New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin is advancing its Blue Moon lunar landers with seven vehicles in production despite recovering from the recent New Glenn rocket explosion. The company remains focused on lunar development amid the incident.

title: Iridium folds Aireon aviation safety service into Rocket Lab-bound business
summary: Iridium Communications has fully acquired Aireon, integrating the aircraft-tracking service into its operations ahead of a planned $8 billion sale to Rocket Lab. The move consolidates Iridium's business as it prepares for the acquisition by Rocket Lab.

title: NASA adds three European firms to the commercial data program
summary: NASA's Commercial Satellite Data Acquisition program (CSDA) awarded contracts to eight new commercial satellite data providers, including three European firms: Kuva Space (Finland) and OroraTech. This expansion aims to enhance NASA's access to commercial Earth observation data.

title: The term ‘dual-use’ is misleading and dangerous
summary: The article argues that the term 'dual-use' is misleading and dangerous, as technology's intent and context depend on political will rather than the technology itself. It emphasizes that a precision optical payload serves its function regardless of its application.

title: BAE microchip designed for strategic defense missions passes radiation test
summary: BAE Systems' Endura microchip has successfully passed radiation tests, making it suitable for classified and missile-defense satellite programs. The company is positioning this technology for strategic defense missions.

title: Connected Aircraft Fleet Set to Exceed 70,000 by 2035
summary: The In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market is projected to see sustained growth, with connected aircraft numbers exceeding 70,000 by 2035. Novaspace's latest report highlights this long-term trend and rising IFC penetration.

title: Centre for Space Futures, Novaspace and SpaceTech Gulf  Sign Agreement to Develop Global Space Capability Mapping Dashboard
summary: The Centre for Space Futures (CSF), Novaspace, and SpaceTech Gulf signed an agreement to develop a global space capability mapping dashboard. The collaboration aims to enhance international space capabilities through data-driven insights.

title: Astronaut flexes his muscles mid-spacewalk | Space photo of the day for July 7, 2026
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams flexed his muscles during a spacewalk with Jessica Meir. The event was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for July 7, 2026.

title: Chinese scientists find the best way to nuke an asteroid on its way to impact Earth
summary: Chinese scientists have proposed two methods to deflect an asteroid on a collision course with Earth. While no immediate threats are identified, the research aims to prepare for potential future scenarios.

title: SpaceX just launched the 1st-ever nuclear-powered commercial satellite
summary: SpaceX launched the first-ever nuclear-powered commercial satellite aboard its Transporter-17 rideshare mission. The spacecraft is the first commercially designed and operated satellite to use nuclear energy in orbit.

title: The sun's atmosphere is way hotter than its surface. Scientists may finally know why
summary: NASA's Parker Solar Probe has discovered charged dust grains in the Sun's outer atmosphere, offering a potential explanation for why the corona is much hotter than the Sun's surface.

title: Even astronauts in space saw America 250 fireworks on the Fourth of July. See their ISS view of Los Angeles (video)
summary: Astronauts aboard the ISS witnessed America's 250th Independence Day fireworks celebrations on July 4th. The event was visible from space, showcasing Los Angeles' festivities.

title: SpaceX launches 81 satellites to orbit from California, lands rocket on ship at sea
summary: SpaceX launched 81 satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket from California. The rocket successfully landed on a drone ship at sea after the mission.

title: More clues surface about the origins of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
summary: Observations of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS using the Very Large Telescope in Chile provide new clues about its origins. The findings align with similar observations made by the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST).

title: NASA just found a planet 'hiding' in TESS spacecraft data, all thanks to Einstein
summary: NASA's TESS spacecraft has discovered a new exoplanet using Einstein's theoretical framework. This method enhances the detection of distant worlds beyond our solar system.

title: Black holes buried in mysterious 'little red dot' galaxies could blast cosmic ghosts at Earth
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has discovered mysterious 'little red dot' galaxies in the early universe that may contain black holes emitting high-energy neutrinos. These cosmic phenomena could potentially blast neutrinos toward Earth.

title: Unidentified metal spheres found on Australian beach are 'debris from a foreign rocket body', space agency says
summary: Unidentified metal spheres found on an Australian beach have been identified as debris from a foreign rocket body by the Australian Space Agency. Residents in Queensland are advised to avoid handling the objects.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is advancing preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are accelerating efforts to meet this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar missions and establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that caused severe damage during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations at the site promptly.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Several orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is scheduled for its first mission since 2021 to air launch the Swift Boost Mission, aiming to save a NASA space telescope. The mission highlights the continued use of air-launched rockets for space operations.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help astronomers study the galaxy's structure and dynamics.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting. These events aim to inform industry about upcoming activities and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential to perform multiple tasks. This innovation could significantly enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a significant step forward for the mission's scientific objectives.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to LEO, including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The mission lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Base at 3:12am.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to support standalone replacements for the ISS by releasing a draft RFP for Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) development. Industry feedback has validated the feasibility of human habitation in LEO.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics has raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals. The company aims to make laser communications more accessible by creating lighter, smaller, and scalable terminals.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article highlights the largest fundraises in the first half of 2026 within the space industry, emphasizing how increased investment benefits the entire sector. It suggests that a surge in funding is driving innovation and growth across global space ventures.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US are fostering stronger space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the global space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly successfully captured and delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC. The rapid execution highlights True Anomaly's operational efficiency.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space for its second military satellite mission named Aurora, which is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The company aims to use lunar dust to build support structures and shielding for reactor cores.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress following a mishap and announced contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims for monthly landings with NASA payloads using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on lunar landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It highlights the progress and preparations for these missions as part of NASA's Artemis program.

title: An Expedition Across the Arctic-Space Connection With David Marsh
summary: David Marsh recently returned from an expedition to the Arctic to study parallels between the hostile environment and space. The journey is discussed in an interview featured on 'On Orbit'.

title: Neo Space and Hughes Deploy 3 Ground Gateways to Support IFC
summary: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems have deployed three new Hughes ground gateways in Saudi Arabia and Europe to support NSG's in-flight connectivity (IFC). This deployment aims to enhance NSG's global connectivity services.
company: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems

title: Univity Signs MoU With Indonesia’s Telkomsat on VLEO and D2D Connectivity
summary: Univity has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Indonesia's Telkomsat to explore collaboration on satellite connectivity, including Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) architecture and Direct-to-Device (D2D) connectivity.

title: Mission to Rescue NASA Swift Observatory Launches
summary: A mission to rescue NASA's Swift Observatory has launched, with Northrop Grumman's Katalyst Space LINK servicing spacecraft successfully making contact. The servicing mission aims to extend the life of the aging observatory.

title: Cyber in Space Signs Partnership Deal with ExeQuantum
summary: Cyber in Space has partnered with ExeQuantum to enhance cyber resilience and quantum-readiness services for the global aerospace and space ecosystem. The collaboration aims to deliver advanced security solutions to organizations in the space industry.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service after a ULA launch. The mission marks a key milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet's next-generation Pelican satellites as early as late 2026. This marks a significant milestone for Isar Aerospace's developing launcher.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intent to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition aims to strengthen Mitsubishi Electric's position in the industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully tested a direct-to-device (D2D) call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation for emergency services connectivity. The test is part of efforts to enhance communication for first responders.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the growing importance of satellite networks for non-government applications, particularly the applications that dominate satellite traffic in space. It explores why this matters as thousands of new satellites are launched into orbit.
